articles.title: Uncertainty and Calibration Under Shift, Noise, and Autocorrelation: A Simulation Benchmark
articles.notes: Simulation benchmark for uncertainty in regression, classification, and forecasting under shift, noise, and temporal dependence.

articles.title: Stress-Testing Online Change-Point Detection Under Missingness, Outliers, and Seasonality: A Simulation Study
articles.notes: Main CPD simulation paper.

articles.title: Geodesic Non-Local Means for Geometry-Aware Image Denoising
articles.notes: Research codebase for geometry-aware image denoising with reliable baselines, graph-based geodesic denoising methods, reproducible experiments, and publication-ready paper artifacts (figures, tables, and LaTeX alignment).

articles.title: Calibration vs accuracy trade-offs
articles.notes: Need to strengthen simulation design and references.

articles.title: Synthetic smart-home stress testing for ML systems
articles.notes: Repository scaffold exists, experiments not complete yet.

articles.title: Curvature-adaptive nearest-neighbour classification
articles.notes: Core repo is in place; reminder issue should be closed.

articles.title: TMLE Missingness Simulation Study
articles.notes: Simulation study comparing MI + Rubin versus missingness-aware orthogonal estimators with bootstrap or BLB inference for causal effects under missing data.

articles.title: pro-particles
articles.notes: NumPy-first interacting-particle sampler for Proper-Scoring-Rule posteriors with log-score and MMD² examples.

articles.title: Curvature-Adaptive k-NN on Nonlinear Manifolds
articles.notes: Research codebase for curvature-adaptive nearest-neighbour classification, including fixed-k, curvature-adaptive, and curvature-weighted k-NN with reproducible synthetic benchmarks, runtime tracking, and paper artifact generation.

articles.title: Economic Crises as Redistribution Operators: A Dynamical Systems Model of Wage Decline, Wealth Concentration, and Policy Control
articles.notes: Reproducible research repo with formal dynamical systems model, ETL pipeline for inequality/macro/labour data, simulation code, empirical panel analysis, and policy counterfactuals. Treats crisis outcomes as government choices.

articles.title: Wealth Concentration as a Destructive Attractor: A Conditional Dynamical-Systems Benchmark
articles.notes: Benchmark two-variable dynamical-systems paper proving conditional local collapse stability under explicit institutional and demand-side thresholds, with equilibrium geometry, basin structure, separatrix analysis, and bifurcation results. Model-internal and non-empirical by design.

articles.title: From Destructive Attractors to Institutional Resilience: Calibration, Stochastic Escape, and Policy Counterfactuals
articles.notes: Extension paper adding public-data calibration, SMM and ABC identification checks, weak-identification diagnostics, three-variable bargaining dynamics, ABM consistency, stochastic escape, EVT-style rare-event analysis, and policy counterfactuals. Uses the benchmark theorem from Paper 1 without re-proving it.

articles.title: From Bargaining Collapse to Institutional Erosion: Higher-Dimensional Destructive Attractor Models
articles.notes: Higher-dimensional theory paper developing the 3D bargaining model and 4D endogenous-institutions model as a hierarchy extending the 2D benchmark. Focuses on forward invariance, equilibria, resilience regions, deterministic simulations, basin slices, and continuation diagnostics.

articles.title: Monte Carlo Cartography of Destructive Attractors: Collapse Probabilities, Resilience Thresholds, and Global Sensitivity
articles.notes: Computational uncertainty paper building a Monte Carlo atlas across the 2D, 3D, and 4D model hierarchy. Covers parameter and initial-condition uncertainty, stochastic shocks, collapse and resilience probability maps, first-passage summaries, and global sensitivity analysis without making empirical-probability claims.

articles.title: Koopman Early-Warning Indicators for Destructive Attractors in Political-Economy Dynamical Systems
articles.notes: Methodological paper applying Koopman operator methods and EDMD to derive slow modes, basin-separating observables, and short-window collapse predictors across the model hierarchy. Emphasizes model-internal diagnostics, spectral comparison, and robustness rather than empirical forecasting.

articles.title: Topological Cartography of Collapse and Resilience: Morse Graphs and Basin Structure in Destructive-Attractor Models
articles.notes: Set-oriented topology paper constructing coarse global state-space summaries through box decompositions, transition graphs, recurrent components, Morse-style graphs, and basin comparisons across 2D, 3D, and 4D models. Claims are explicitly resolution- and flow-time-dependent rather than theorem-level topology statements.

articles.title: Open-Access Municipal Housing Price Dynamics in Portugal: Replication and Multi-Layer Extension Scaffolds
articles.notes: Open-access Portuguese municipal housing research pipeline that reproduces the 2017 cross-sectional housing-price model and extends it with auditable ETL, validation, model-ready datasets, panel econometrics, spatial diagnostics and spatial models, RNAL exposure collection, causal scaffolds, granular data architecture, and temporal clustering. The repository is explicit that substantive empirical claims depend on official input data and that spatial, clustering, and causal layers have distinct inferential status.

articles.title: From Chaos to Crisis: Using Dynamical-Systems Extreme Value Theory in Econometrics
articles.notes: Applies extreme value theory from dynamical systems (Milhazes Freitas framework) to econometric analysis of rare events, crises, and tail dependence. Includes simulation utilities for chaotic maps, rare-event diagnostics (exceedances, extremal index, return/hitting times), multivariate stress analysis, and reproduction package with YAML-based experiment orchestration. Designed for both Medium article and future formal paper.

articles.title: From Wages to Wealth: A Dynamical Model and Research Design for Rent Extraction, Property Ownership, and Class Reproduction
articles.notes: Develops a political-economy model of wealth concentration centered on the conversion of wages into asset ownership, with housing as the main transmission channel. Includes a deterministic dynamic model with explicit ownership-transition timing and affordability constraints, Portugal-oriented calibration scenarios, fixed-effects panel utilities, household ownership-transition modeling, dashboards, notebooks, and a reproducible simulation pipeline. The current empirical layer is implemented in code but still relies on synthetic sample panel data and provisional calibration anchors, so the repo is best understood as a theory-plus-research-design package for a working paper and future formal article.

articles.title: The European Economy as a Set of Choices: A Reusable Diagnostic Framework for Convergence, Wages, Investment, and Fiscal Policy
articles.notes: Policy-facing analytical framework for diagnosing the European economy through economic choices rather than headline GDP. Implements a synthetic EU macro-panel generator, growth and beta/sigma convergence analysis, wage-productivity gap and labour/profit-share diagnostics, public and private investment indicators, fiscal-stance classification, inflation and real-wage pressure measures, inequality indicators, country scorecards, and an automated Portugal-vs-EU policy brief, exposed through a Typer CLI, notebooks, and tests. The framework currently runs offline on synthetic panels with an explicit extension path to Eurostat, AMECO, and ECB data, so it is best understood as a reusable diagnostic toolkit awaiting connection to official sources.

articles.title: Has Supply-Side Liberalisation Been Tested? Event Studies, Synthetic Control, and Panel Evidence on Its Promised Gains
articles.notes: Reproducible empirical project that treats the supply-side liberalisation package (lower income and corporate taxes, privatisation, labour-market flexibility, a smaller public sector) as a falsifiable claim and tests whether it historically delivered higher productivity, real wages, GDP per capita, and lower poverty. Combines event studies around policy episodes (Portugal 2011-2015, Kansas 2012-2017, UK post-1979, US post-1981, Eastern European flat-tax reforms), a synthetic-control design for Portugal, and OECD/EU panel models, alongside an indicator contract, source registry, methodology notes, notebook templates, a Django dashboard scaffold, and tests. The analytical scaffolding, reusable functions, and agent prompts are in place, but the data providers are not yet fully connected, so the empirical conclusions remain to be produced under the repository's explicit robustness and failure-criteria rules.

articles.title: Could Portugal Build a Sovereign Fund from Social Security Surpluses? A Monte Carlo Stress-Testing Framework
articles.notes: Transparent Monte Carlo stress-testing framework (explicitly not a forecast) for whether Portugal could build a sovereign or strategic fund via the indirect route of Social Security surpluses, FEFSS transfers, and financial-asset purchases. Anchored on editable 2025 fiscal and FEFSS baselines, it simulates public-debt trajectories, FEFSS pension-coverage ratios, sovereign-fund asset growth, and downside risk across six policy scenarios spanning conservative reinforcement, debt-reduction-first, higher-equity mandates, indirect and aggressive strategic funds, and a post-2035 demographic-stress case. A second engine isolates the specific risk of buying Portuguese equities with pension surpluses, using transparent proxy returns by default and optional downloaded market data. The model deliberately separates fiscal balance, financial allocation, and mandate, and is designed to be falsifiable and improvable with better demographic and return calibration.

articles.title: From Employer Wages to State Transfers: A Ten-Paper Programme on Poverty, the Low-Wage Subsidy State, and Asset Power in the US and UK Since 1950
articles.notes: Agent-first scaffold for a sequenced ten-paper empirical research programme sharing one data pipeline, modelling framework, set of definitions, and reproducibility contract. It tests the working hypothesis that since the Reagan and Thatcher periods poverty reduction shifted away from employer responsibility and wage growth toward state correction through taxes and transfers, while the upper tail increasingly captured income via assets, business ownership, and capital gains taxed differently from wages - that is, that public budgets increasingly subsidised low wages as asset owners captured a growing share of income and wealth. The papers span the historical poverty record, lagged policy effects, market versus state-corrected poverty, the low-wage subsidy state, the incidence of poverty reduction, asset income and top wealth, labour power, housing, synthetic counterfactuals, and a final synthesis. The specification, data contracts, and per-paper prompts are complete; the empirical work is deliberately left minimal for a coding agent to implement, and the repo is explicit that the hypothesis must be tested rather than assumed and that synthetic data must not be used as evidence.

articles.title: From Wages to Wealth: A Dynamical Model and Research Design for Rent Extraction, Property Ownership, and Class Reproduction
articles.notes: Develops a political-economy model of wealth concentration centered on the conversion of wages into asset ownership, with housing as the main transmission channel. Includes a deterministic dynamic model with explicit ownership-transition timing and affordability constraints, Portugal-oriented calibration scenarios, fixed-effects panel utilities, household ownership-transition modeling, dashboards, notebooks, and a reproducible simulation pipeline. The current empirical layer is implemented in code but still relies on synthetic sample panel data and provisional calibration anchors, so the repo is best understood as a theory-plus-research-design package for a working paper and future formal article.

articles.title: North–South Fire-Season Onset in Mainland Portugal: A Reproducible Data-Engineering and Statistics Pipeline
articles.notes: Tests whether rural fire onset is earlier at higher latitude, keeping ignition, burned-area, and fire-weather onset separate. Multi-source ingestion (ICNF, NASA FIRMS, EFFIS, ERA5/CEMS, GISCO, CORINE/DGT COS, DEM, OSM, INE) via a provenance-tracked download manager into PostGIS. Pure-Python statistics (OLS+robust SE, sign test, permutation, discrete-time hazard, Cox PH, Weibull AFT, random-intercept mixed, quantile, robustness). All 29 prompts implemented; ruff+mypy clean; 197 unit tests; offline path (features onset -> stats run -> viz build) reproducible. Remaining: live credentialed downloads (FIRMS/CDS), live PostGIS loader run, real GeoTIFF raster reads. Article is a scaffold, not yet written.

articles.title: Can Portugal Produce a Parish-Level Map of Rent per Square Metre? A Coverage and Concordance Audit of Official INE Rent Data
articles.notes: Reproducible pipeline auditing whether a national parish-level (freguesia) rent-per-m2 map can be built from observed official INE data alone, with explicit coverage reporting, CAOP 2025 concordance, and strict separation of official / asking-price / inferred / modelled layers.
